gitCommit.subject: fix(product_images): close GAP-J5, GAP-C5, GAP-C5b, GAP-C6 + C16 invariant (#944)
gitCommit.body:
fix(product_images): close GAP-J5, GAP-C5, GAP-C5b, GAP-C6 + C16 invariant (#944)

* feat(product_images): CF×PI reconciliation 10/10 — melhorias M1–M12

Todas as 15 invariantes do health check passam (C01–C15: OK).

Migrations aplicadas em doufsxqlfjyuvxuezpln (2026-06-18):
- M01: Link 2.485 deps em 1.621 grupos por content_hash
- M02: RLS em 6 tabelas cf_recon (cf_image, ghost_check_queue, action_log, crawl_run, metric_snapshot, remediation)
- M03: Corrige 1.146 is_shared=true sem canonical_image_id
- M04: Re-sync primary_image_url de 1 produto divergente
- M05: Limpeza de 720 itens done na backfill_queue
- M06: Backfill last_modified_source=pipeline em 66.794 linhas
- M07: v_cf_drift_dashboard v2 (21 métricas: canonical, blurhash, content_hash)
- M08: fn_autolink_canonical_on_content_hash + trigger (dedup automático futuro)
- M09: reconcile_cf_image_status() — função SECURITY DEFINER para edge function
- M10: fn_product_images_health_check() — 15 invariantes C01–C15
- M11: Flatten 65 cadeias canônicas A→B→C → A→C
- M12: Corrige 65 artefactos de CTE (is_shared=true, canonical=NULL)

Estado final:
  recon_total=72079, pi_active_total=72079, pi_verified=72079 (100.00%)
  pi_orphaned=0, pi_missing=0, cf_orphans_not_in_pi=0
  pi_shared_no_canonical=0, canonical_chains_flat=OK

https://claude.ai/code/session_0114PNmsRZRNoH9MLGvfivET

* feat(product_images): close 3 canonical gaps found in deep simulation round 2

Simulation of 200+ scenarios across 12 attack categories revealed 3 real gaps:

- GAP-B6: Restoration of a soft-deleted dep whose canonical was subsequently
  deleted created a C13 violation (live image pointing to deleted root).
  Fix: trg_handle_image_restoration clears stale canonical on restoration.

- GAP-C3: Changing content_hash on an already-linked dep did not re-link to
  the new hash group (trigger skipped due to non-NULL canonical check).
  Fix: fn_autolink_canonical_on_content_hash now detects hash change on
  linked dep, clears old canonical, and re-links to the correct new root.

- GAP-D: Direct UPDATE of canonical_image_id to a dep (non-root) created
  chains violating C07. Health check detected but did not prevent.
  Fix: trg_prevent_canonical_chain auto-flattens any chain attempt silently.

Also adds idx_pi_canonical_active for optimal trigger query performance.
Health check: 15/15 OK post-deployment. All 3 gaps verified closed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0114PNmsRZRNoH9MLGvfivET

* feat(product_images): close GAP-J — chain creation via INSERT now auto-flattened

Adds trg_prevent_canonical_chain_insert (BEFORE INSERT) using the existing
fn_prevent_canonical_chain function. Previously, fn_prevent_canonical_chain
only fired on UPDATE OF canonical_image_id, leaving a gap where a direct
INSERT with canonical_image_id pointing to a dep (non-root) could create a
multi-level chain, violating C07.

Verified via simulation round 2 scenario J:
  sim-j2-chain inserted with canonical→sim-j2-dep, trigger flattened to root.

Health check: 15/15 OK after fix.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0114PNmsRZRNoH9MLGvfivET

* fix(product_images): close GAP-J5, GAP-C5, GAP-C5b, GAP-C6 + add C16 invariant

5 migrations from exhaustive simulation (250+ scenarios, 40 batteries):

Migration 005 — fix_gaps_j5_and_c6:
- GAP-J5: fn_prevent_canonical_chain did not guard against soft-deleted targets.
  Both INSERT and UPDATE paths could create dep→deleted_root (C13 violation).
  Fix: check deleted_at of target canonical; clear if soft-deleted.
- GAP-C6: fn_autolink first-set branch respected existing canonical even when it
  pointed to a different-hash group. Fix: validate canonical's content_hash matches
  NEW.content_hash; if mismatch, fall through to re-link to correct group.

Migration 006 — fix_gap_c5_add_c16:
- Adds C16 health-check invariant: deps whose content_hash ≠ canonical's content_hash.
- Also adds AFTER trigger for GAP-C5 (later found defective; dropped in 007).

Migration 007 — fix_gap_c5_embed_relink:
- Drops defective AFTER trigger (column-specific AFTER triggers do not fire when
  BEFORE triggers modify that column, not the original SET list).
- Embeds GAP-C5 former-dep re-linking directly inside the BEFORE trigger.
- Defect: re-linked deps to new root regardless of their OWN hash (C16 violations).

Migration 008 — fix_gap_c5_correct_relink:
- Corrects re-linking: former deps re-grouped by THEIR OWN content_hash.
  Case A: another live root exists for OLD.content_hash → link all deps to it.
  Case B: no other root → elect oldest former dep as new root, link remaining.
- Defect: C5 block gated on v_root_id IS NOT NULL, skipping when root stays
  standalone in new group (GAP-C5b — C16 violation in that case).

Migration 009 — fix_gap_c5b_new_group:
- Broadens C5 condition from (OLD.canonical_image_id IS NULL AND v_root_id IS NOT NULL)
  to (OLD.canonical_image_id IS NULL AND OLD.content_hash IS NOT NULL).
  This fires whenever the row WAS a root in a previous hash group, regardless
  of whether it joins an existing group or stays standalone in a new group.

Health check post-all-fixes: C07/C08/C09/C13/C16 all OK (0 violations).

https://claude.ai/code/session_0114PNmsRZRNoH9MLGvfivET

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(product_images): close GAP-J — chain creation via INSERT now auto-flattened

Adds trg_prevent_canonical_chain_insert (BEFORE INSERT) using the existing
fn_prevent_canonical_chain function. Previously, fn_prevent_canonical_chain
only fired on UPDATE OF canonical_image_id, leaving a gap where a direct
INSERT with canonical_image_id pointing to a dep (non-root) could create a
multi-level chain, violating C07.

Verified via simulation round 2 scenario J:
  sim-j2-chain inserted with canonical→sim-j2-dep, trigger flattened to root.

Health check: 15/15 OK after fix.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0114PNmsRZRNoH9MLGvfivET

* fix(product_images): close GAP-J5, GAP-C5, GAP-C5b, GAP-C6 + add C16 invariant

5 migrations from exhaustive simulation (250+ scenarios, 40 batteries):

Migration 005 — fix_gaps_j5_and_c6:
- GAP-J5: fn_prevent_canonical_chain did not guard against soft-deleted targets.
  Both INSERT and UPDATE paths could create dep→deleted_root (C13 violation).
  Fix: check deleted_at of target canonical; clear if soft-deleted.
- GAP-C6: fn_autolink first-set branch respected existing canonical even when it
  pointed to a different-hash group. Fix: validate canonical's content_hash matches
  NEW.content_hash; if mismatch, fall through to re-link to correct group.

Migration 006 — fix_gap_c5_add_c16:
- Adds C16 health-check invariant: deps whose content_hash ≠ canonical's content_hash.
- Also adds AFTER trigger for GAP-C5 (later found defective; dropped in 007).

Migration 007 — fix_gap_c5_embed_relink:
- Drops defective AFTER trigger (column-specific AFTER triggers do not fire when
  BEFORE triggers modify that column, not the original SET list).
- Embeds GAP-C5 former-dep re-linking directly inside the BEFORE trigger.
- Defect: re-linked deps to new root regardless of their OWN hash (C16 violations).

Migration 008 — fix_gap_c5_correct_relink:
- Corrects re-linking: former deps re-grouped by THEIR OWN content_hash.
  Case A: another live root exists for OLD.content_hash → link all deps to it.
  Case B: no other root → elect oldest former dep as new root, link remaining.
- Defect: C5 block gated on v_root_id IS NOT NULL, skipping when root stays
  standalone in new group (GAP-C5b — C16 violation in that case).

Migration 009 — fix_gap_c5b_new_group:
- Broadens C5 condition from (OLD.canonical_image_id IS NULL AND v_root_id IS NOT NULL)
  to (OLD.canonical_image_id IS NULL AND OLD.content_hash IS NOT NULL).
  This fires whenever the row WAS a root in a previous hash group, regardless
  of whether it joins an existing group or stays standalone in a new group.

Health check post-all-fixes: C07/C08/C09/C13/C16 all OK (0 violations).

https://claude.ai/code/session_0114PNmsRZRNoH9MLGvfivET

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(product_images): close 3 canonical gaps found in deep simulation round 2

Simulation of 200+ scenarios across 12 attack categories revealed 3 real gaps:

- GAP-B6: Restoration of a soft-deleted dep whose canonical was subsequently
  deleted created a C13 violation (live image pointing to deleted root).
  Fix: trg_handle_image_restoration clears stale canonical on restoration.

- GAP-C3: Changing content_hash on an already-linked dep did not re-link to
  the new hash group (trigger skipped due to non-NULL canonical check).
  Fix: fn_autolink_canonical_on_content_hash now detects hash change on
  linked dep, clears old canonical, and re-links to the correct new root.

- GAP-D: Direct UPDATE of canonical_image_id to a dep (non-root) created
  chains violating C07. Health check detected but did not prevent.
  Fix: trg_prevent_canonical_chain auto-flattens any chain attempt silently.

Also adds idx_pi_canonical_active for optimal trigger query performance.
Health check: 15/15 OK post-deployment. All 3 gaps verified closed.

  sim-j2-chain inserted with canonical→sim-j2-dep, trigger flattened to root.
